features.description: User persona QA audit: Pretend you are Sarah, a senior PM at a Series B startup who listens to Lenny's Podcast on her commute. She has 5 minutes to explore this site after seeing it shared in Lenny's Slack. Open the homepage (read app/page.tsx and all components it renders). Walk through EVERY page a real user would visit in their first 5 minutes. For each page, note: (1) What's confusing or broken? (2) What's missing? (3) What delights? Document your findings in content/qa/user-persona-audit.md with sections: First Impressions, Navigation Flow, Track Page Experience, Album Page Experience, Search Experience, Mobile Concerns, Top 5 Issues (ranked by impact on first-time visitor). Read EVERY component and page file — app/page.tsx, app/album/[slug]/AlbumPageClient.tsx, app/track/[slug]/TrackPageClient.tsx, app/songs/page.tsx, components/NowPlayingBar.tsx, components/SyncedLyrics.tsx, components/TrackRow.tsx, components/VoteButtons.tsx. Be brutally honest. DONE: content/qa/user-persona-audit.md exists with detailed findings. DO NOT TOUCH: any code files. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.

Audit ALL track annotations for quality and accuracy. Read lib/albums.ts and check every track that has an `annotations` array. For each annotation, verify: (1) The lyricText actually appears as a substring in that track's lyrics. (2) The note is specific, factual, and references real Lenny content — not vague or generic. (3) The sourceTitle and sourceUrl are plausible. Flag any annotations that feel force-fit (where the lyric doesn't clearly reference the annotated concept). Write findings to content/qa/annotation-audit.md. Format: one section per album, with each track listing its annotations and a verdict (GOOD, NEEDS WORK, FORCE-FIT). For FORCE-FIT annotations, suggest a better lyric line to annotate or suggest removing it. DONE: content/qa/annotation-audit.md exists with findings for all annotated tracks. DO NOT TOUCH: lib/albums.ts or any code. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Read lib/albums.ts — find all tracks with annotations
For each annotated track: check lyrics contain lyricText
Evaluate note quality — specific/factual vs vague/generic

features.description: Fix any FORCE-FIT annotations found in the audit (task 2). Read content/qa/annotation-audit.md for the list of problematic annotations. For each FORCE-FIT annotation in lib/albums.ts: either (1) find a better lyric line to match and update lyricText, or (2) rewrite the note to better connect the lyric to the concept, or (3) remove the annotation entirely if it can't be saved. Use the track's lyrics, concept, storyBehind, and keyInsight fields to find better matches. Every remaining annotation must feel natural — like a Genius annotation where you read it and think 'oh that's what that line means!' DONE: No more force-fit annotations. Every annotation feels earned. DO NOT TOUCH: component files, non-annotation track data. VERIFY: pnpm build passes. Each lyricText still appears in its track's lyrics.

Fix top issues from the user persona audit (task 1). Read content/qa/user-persona-audit.md for the Top 5 Issues list. Implement fixes for the top 3 issues that are code-fixable (skip any that require new assets, Eric's input, or major architecture changes). For each fix: read the relevant file, make the minimal change, verify build passes. DONE: Top 3 code-fixable issues from the audit are resolved. DO NOT TOUCH: lib/audio-context.tsx (read only), track data in lib/albums.ts (unless fixing display bugs). VERIFY: pnpm build passes.

Second persona audit: Now pretend you are Marcus, a tech journalist who covers AI/creator tools. He's evaluating LGH as a potential story angle. He cares about: Is this impressive technically? Is it novel? Would his readers care? Walk through the site again with fresh eyes after the fixes from task 4. Read all page files again. Write content/qa/journalist-persona-audit.md with: Headline Angle (what would the story be?), Wow Moments, Credibility Gaps, Missing Polish, Suggested Improvements (max 5, ranked). DONE: content/qa/journalist-persona-audit.md exists. DO NOT TOUCH: any code. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Read all page and component files again

features.description: Fix top issues from the journalist persona audit (task 5). Read content/qa/journalist-persona-audit.md Suggested Improvements. Implement the top 2 code-fixable improvements. Keep changes minimal and focused. DONE: Top 2 code-fixable improvements implemented. DO NOT TOUCH: lib/audio-context.tsx, track data unless fixing display bugs. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.

Third persona audit: Lenny Rachitsky himself. He built the newsletter and podcast this app is based on. He cares about: Does it represent his content accurately? Is it respectful? Would he be proud to share it? Would it make him smile? Read lib/albums.ts to check how his content is represented — track titles, concepts, source attributions, key quotes. Read a few track page files. Write content/qa/lenny-persona-audit.md with: What Would Make Lenny Smile, What Might Concern Lenny, Accuracy Check (any misattributions or misrepresentations?), Missing Content (notable Lenny episodes/topics not covered), Overall Verdict. DONE: content/qa/lenny-persona-audit.md exists. DO NOT TOUCH: any code. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Read lib/albums.ts thoroughly — all track data
Check source attributions, quotes, concepts for accuracy

features.description: Fix any accuracy issues from the Lenny persona audit (task 7). Read content/qa/lenny-persona-audit.md. If there are misattributions, wrong source titles, inaccurate quotes, or misrepresented concepts in lib/albums.ts — fix them. Use mcp__lennysdata__search_content and mcp__lennysdata__read_excerpt to verify facts against the actual Lenny archive. Only fix things that are clearly wrong, not style preferences. DONE: All factual accuracy issues resolved. DO NOT TOUCH: component files. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
steps: Use Lenny MCP to verify facts
